design_philosophy.core_loop: Race against AI opponents by either completing the course, destroying all competitor vehicles, or killing all pedestrians on the level within time limit. Gain time bonuses by damaging cars and running over pedestrians.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure balanced against multiple win conditions. Player must decide between racing the course traditionally or engaging in vehicular destruction and pedestrian carnage.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Unrestrained vehicular mayhem and destruction without rules or consequences; the antithesis of traditional racing games that penalize crashes.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Carmageddon is deliberately provocative. Developer Patrick Buckland noted the team got bored playing traditional racing games and decided to make one where crashing and destroying is the objective. The game explicitly awards points for pedestrian kills—a design choice SCi suggested specifically to maximize controversy. This intentional provocation (running over pedestrians for points) distinguishes it from earlier destructive racing and separates it philosophically from contemporaries like Destruction Derby, which focused only on vehicle-to-vehicle combat.

difficulty.checkpointing: Time-based checkpoint system: player has limited time per race, can gain additional time through bonuses, vehicle destruction, and pedestrian kills
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency in difficulty approach. Player can choose to complete races traditionally (racing line focus) or adopt destructive strategies (targeting pedestrians and opponent vehicles for time bonuses). Multiple win conditions provide flexible difficulty scaling.

progression.upgrade_system: Vehicle selection system. Game includes multiple selectable vehicles (Die Anna featured in documentation) with implicit performance differences, though exact upgrade mechanics not detailed in source.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression through 36 racetracks across 11 different locations. Locations include city, mine, and industrial areas. Sequential level unlocking implied by track progression structure.
replayability_hooks: Multiple vehicle options with different handling characteristics
Three alternative win conditions per race (complete course, wreck all competitors, kill all pedestrians)
Expansion pack (Splat Pack) added new tracks, vehicles, and environments
Network multiplayer levels in expansion pack

world_building_techniques: Environmental theming: distinct location types (city, mine, industrial areas) with visual differentiation
License origin: originally prototyped as Death Race 2000 tie-in, establishing thematic connection to transgressive vehicular fiction

danger_signals: Explicit reward system for pedestrian kills
Intentional design decision to maximize controversy through pedestrian mechanics
Media controversy anticipated and capitalized upon by developer
environmental_storytelling: Industrial and mine settings create distinct play areas with environmental hazards
